data.title: Elon Musk's X Money App Begins Limited Beta Testing with Cashback and Yield Features
data.description:
Elon Musk's new payment application, X Money, has initiated limited external beta testing this week, showcasing features such as cashback and yield on deposits. According to Cointelegraph, Hollywood actor William Shatner, known for his role as Captain Kirk in the original Star Trek series, is among the beta testers invited to participate. Screenshots shared by Shatner reveal that X Money users can earn cashback on specific card purchases and receive a 6% annual percentage yield on deposits.

Deposits within X Money are held by Cross River Bank, a member of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, ensuring insurance coverage up to $250,000 per person. X Money is part of Musk's ambitious vision to transform X into an 'everything app,' encompassing payments, private messaging, AI chatbot services through Grok, creator content, identity, and more. Musk described the app as a 'game changer,' aiming to centralize all monetary transactions.

Shatner has utilized the $42 sent to him by Musk to raise funds for charity, auctioning 42 X Money beta invites for $1,000 each with X's permission. Each winning bidder receives a $25 welcome gift card from X and $1 initially sent to Shatner by Musk. Following the first auction, Shatner and X conducted a second round, auctioning another 166 beta invites at the same price. Eligibility requires users to be U.S. residents over 18 with an active X account in good standing.

Despite Musk's known appreciation for Dogecoin (DOGE), there is no indication of crypto integration into X Money. The app marks Musk's return to the payment space, having founded X.com in the late 1990s before its merger into PayPal. In recent years, X has obtained money transmitter licenses in over 40 U.S. states and registered with the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network to facilitate peer-to-peer payments on the platform.

data.title: Tech Companies Urge Trump to Reassess Anthropic's National Security Risk Designation
data.description: Tech industry groups, representing major companies such as Google and Apple, are calling on U.S. President Donald Trump to reevaluate the decision to label Anthropic as a national security threat. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the concerns raised by these groups about the potential implications of this designation on the tech sector. The industry representatives argue that the move could have significant repercussions for innovation and collaboration within the technology field. They emphasize the importance of maintaining a balanced approach to national security that does not hinder technological progress. The appeal to the President underscores the ongoing dialogue between the tech industry and the government regarding security and innovation.

data.title: Understanding Market Signals: A Rational Approach to Crypto Predictions
data.description:
Crypto KOL Murphy posted on X, expressing appreciation for the positive feedback on the effectiveness of institutional signal BRS. He noted that while the immediate impact of signals, such as 'morning post, afternoon rise,' is gratifying, it is often a matter of luck and coincidence. Murphy emphasized that no single indicator or signal can predict market trends with 100% accuracy; rather, they offer probabilities.

Murphy encouraged followers to focus on the data changes and underlying logic that influence market movements in the short, medium, and long term. He has been analyzing various aspects such as changes in long-term holder behavior, gaps in chip structure, real cost pressures, and extreme market sentiment. These analyses are not based on luck but on objective and rational assessments.

Murphy cautioned against focusing solely on predictions of price movements and the correctness of opinions, as this can lead to fluctuating perceptions of his expertise. He stressed that no one can foresee the future, but experience, knowledge, and methodology can provide insights. Murphy believes that these insights are accessible to everyone willing to learn and understand the market dynamics.

data.title: Valence Introduces Trading Superapp for Prediction Markets
data.description: Y Combinator posted on X about Valence, a new trading superapp designed for prediction markets. The platform allows users to trade and execute strategies across multiple exchanges through a single interface. Valence offers a unified orderbook and API, facilitating streamlined trading operations. The app has already seen over 1 billion contracts traded, highlighting its growing popularity and utility in the trading community.

data.title: German robotics startup Neura has raised approximately €1 billion in funding, with Tether participating.
data.description: German robotics startup Neura Robotics is raising approximately €1 billion (about $1.2 billion) in funding, with stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA participating. The funds will be used to develop AI-driven humanoid robots. This round values ​​the company at approximately €4 billion, with further funding potentially possible. Headquartered in Metzingen, Germany, Neura Robotics currently sells factory transport robots and robotic arms for home use. CEO David Reger previously stated that the company received nearly $1 billion in orders last year, with clients including Japanese companies Kawasaki Heavy Industries and Omron. In January 2025, the company completed a €120 million funding round led by Exor's Lingotto Investment Management. This investment is the latest move by Tether to expand beyond its crypto business; Tether has previously invested in brain-computer interface company Blackrock Neurotech and Italian robotics startup Generative Bionics.

data.title: CZ: Buying up UAE real estate at rock-bottom prices?
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform that he had discovered an interesting website and asked, "Should I buy (UAE property at rock-bottom prices)?", while clarifying that this was not financial advice. The post referenced a tweet from Panic Selling, which announced that its platform now tracks the luxury rental market in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, stating that it will notify users immediately if landlords lower prices. Panic Selling reportedly scans over 20,000 UAE luxury property listings daily and plans to expand to more cities. CZ has followed the account.

data.title: US stocks closed with a strong rebound in the crypto sector, with Coinbase rising over 14%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Nasdaq rose 1.29%, the S&P 500 rose 0.78%, and the Dow Jones rose 0.49%. The crypto sector collectively strengthened, with Solana rising 16.67%, Coinbase rising 14.57%, SharpLink rising 11.98%, Strategy (MSTR) rising 10.37%, Bitmine rising 7.92%, and Circle rising 5.66%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: A deputy to the National People's Congress stated that China's independently developed blockchain underlying technology has been applied in 16 central ministries and 27 central enterprises.
data.description: Dong Jin, a deputy to the National People's Congress and president of the Beijing Microchip Blockchain and Edge Computing Research Institute, stated that my country's independently developed blockchain underlying technology has been applied to 16 central ministries and 27 central enterprises, achieving positive progress in areas such as taxation, cross-border trade, and global payments. Tens of billions of invoices are processed annually on my country's domestically developed blockchain, with over 300,000 enterprises involved in cross-border trade on the blockchain, representing a trade volume of trillions of yuan. (CCTV News)

data.title: SEC Advances on Digital Asset Regulations Amid Legislative Uncertainty
data.description:
Nate Geraci, president of the ETF Store, posted on X that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is not waiting for legislative clarity to advance its regulatory framework for digital assets. Despite the absence of a comprehensive Clarity Act to classify crypto assets, the SEC is reportedly prepared to utilize its existing authorities to establish rules for digital assets.

The SEC's proactive stance comes amid ongoing debates among politicians on how to effectively regulate the burgeoning crypto market. The agency's decision to move forward highlights its commitment to addressing regulatory gaps and ensuring investor protection in the rapidly evolving digital asset space.

Industry experts have noted that while legislative action would provide a more structured approach, the SEC's initiative could offer interim solutions to pressing regulatory challenges. The move is seen as a significant step in providing clearer guidelines for market participants and enhancing the overall stability of the crypto market.

data.title: Anthropic CEO Engages with U.S. Defense Officials on Military Tech Applications
data.description: Anthropic's CEO is reportedly in discussions with a deputy to U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to explore potential compromises regarding the use of Anthropic's technology in military applications. According to Jin10, these talks aim to address how the company's advancements can be integrated into defense strategies while balancing ethical considerations. The outcome of these discussions could influence the future role of AI technologies in military operations, highlighting the growing intersection between tech companies and defense sectors.

data.title: Helius Launches Developer Toolkit for Solana Infrastructure
data.description: Helius, a provider of Solana infrastructure, has introduced a new toolkit aimed at developers. According to Foresight News, the suite includes the Helius MCP server, Helius skill library, Helius command line interface, and Helius Claude code plugin. The team has also enhanced the TypeScript and Rust SDKs along with their documentation system.

data.title: SOL Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow on March 4
data.description:
On March 5, it was reported that SOL spot ETF experienced a substantial net inflow of $19.063 million on March 4, Eastern Time. According to PANews, the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) was the sole contributor to this inflow, bringing its historical total net inflow to $35.0845 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of the SOL spot ETF stands at $900 million, with a net asset ratio of 1.70%. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $971 million.

data.title: The crypto market rebounded broadly, with the RWA sector rising over 7% and BTC breaking through $74,000 intraday.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market saw a broad-based rally. Bitcoin (BTC) rose 6.85%, surpassing $72,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 7.45%, surpassing $2,100. The RWA sector performed exceptionally well, rising 7.26%, with MANTRA (MANTRA) rising 39.03% and Sky (SKY) rising 7.58%. Notably, MAG7.ssi rose 6.40%, DEFI.ssi rose 4.49%, and MEME.ssi rose 7.49%. In other sectors, the Meme sector rose 5.68% in the last 24 hours, with BUILDon (B) up 13.20%; the PayFi sector rose 4.92%, with Telcoin (TEL) up 7.64%; the DeFi sector rose 3.85%, with CoW Protocol (COW) up 8.96%; the CeFi sector rose 3.72%, with Gate (GT) up 4.18%; the Layer 1 sector rose 3.56%, with Decred (DCR) up 11.61%; the Layer 2 sector rose 1.89%, with Stacks (STX) up 5.08%; and the AI ​​sector rose 1.70%, with Venice Token (VVV) up 15.51%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiRWA, ssiMeme, and ssiLayer1 indices rose 6.57%, 5.17%, and 4.71%, respectively.

data.title: The US Senate has lost another crypto tax reform advocate; Daines has confirmed he will not seek re-election.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett reported on the X platform that another major cryptocurrency tax reform advocate in the US Senate will not seek re-election. Senators Lummis and Steve Daines have already confirmed they will not run for re-election, and Marsha Blackburn, if elected governor of Tennessee, will also leave the Senate.

data.title: Gold and silver prices rose together, while European stocks strengthened and the US dollar came under pressure.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices rose to $5,176.92 per ounce, a daily increase of 0.69%. Silver prices rose to $84.525 per ounce, a daily increase of 1.21%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 55.03, a daily decrease of 0.83%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 74.65, a daily increase of 0.89%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate fell 0.03% to 6.88711. The USD/JPY exchange rate fell 0.24% to 156.673. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 rose 1.19% to 5,891.39 points; the UK FTSE 100 fell 0.31% to 10,576.17 points; and the German DAX 40 rose 0.99% to 24,255.88 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 0.69% to $76.68 per barrel, while Brent crude oil rose 0.54% to $83.37 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: Hong Kong AI Stocks Surge as MINIMAX-WP Rises Nearly 13%
data.description: Hong Kong's AI application stocks experienced significant gains, with MINIMAX-WP (00100.HK) leading the charge by rising nearly 13%. According to Jin10, other notable performers included WeRide-W (00800.HK) and Mobvista (01860.HK), both increasing by over 5%. Additionally, Pony.ai-W (02026.HK) saw a rise of more than 4%, reflecting growing investor interest in AI technologies.

data.title: Major Ethereum Bull Expands Bitcoin Position on Hyperliquid
data.description: On March 5, a significant Ethereum investor on Hyperliquid increased their Bitcoin holdings, according to BlockBeats. The investor added to their Bitcoin long position at an average price of $68,178, bringing the total value to $47.41 million. This move has resulted in an unrealized profit of $3.1 million. Additionally, the investor holds a long position in 120,000 ETH, with an unrealized profit of $16.2 million. Overall, the total unrealized profit from these positions amounts to $19.3 million.

data.title: Solana's Loopscale Integrates Orca and Raydium LP Tokens for Collateral Use
data.description:
Solana-based lending protocol Loopscale has announced that LP tokens from Orca and Raydium can now be used as collateral. According to BlockBeats, Loopscale's order book architecture allows for pricing based on the specific conditions of each position, rather than incorporating them into a centralized lending pool model.

This credit market infrastructure unlocks over $1 billion in yield liquidity. The system independently evaluates and prices each LP based on multiple variables, including price range, fee levels, and underlying assets.

data.title: UK and US Form Transatlantic Future Markets Task Force
data.description:
The UK Treasury announced on Monday the formation of a "Transatlantic Future Markets Task Force" with the U.S. government. According to ChainCatcher, the initiative aims to streamline procedures for businesses entering the capital markets of both countries and enhance cooperation in the crypto asset sector.

The task force is expected to deliver a report within 180 days, exploring short-term improvements and long-term options, particularly in the wholesale digital markets. This move has been approved by UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves and U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Besant. The task force will be co-chaired by officials from both countries' finance ministries and will include participation from regulatory agencies.

data.title: Iranian Su-24 Bomber Destroyed Minutes Before Planned Attack on U.S. Base
data.description: An Iranian Su-24 bomber was reportedly destroyed just minutes before it was set to attack the largest U.S. military base in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the incident occurred amidst heightened tensions in the region. The destruction of the aircraft prevented a potential escalation of conflict between Iran and the United States. The U.S. military remains on high alert as geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve in the Middle East.
